summary: WASI project audit reveals critical build failure and API routing inconsistencies. Core functionality (WASI terminal, Banking app, DEX app) is implemented and routes work correctly. DEX backend endpoints are functional with 42 ETF catalog properly seeded. However, npm build failure blocks production deployment, and there's a typo in the DEX order form causing runtime errors.

critical.issue: npm build command fails with exit code -1
critical.why: Blocks production deployment and indicates build configuration issues
critical.fix: Investigate build failure by running 'npm run build' with verbose output, check for missing dependencies, environment variables, or Vite configuration errors

critical.issue: Typo in DEX order form select element
critical.why: Causes runtime error when placing orders, breaking core DEX functionality
critical.fix: Change 'orderFor' to 'orderForm' in the select element value attribute around line 560

high.issue: Inconsistent API URL resolution between DEX and WASI modules
high.why: Risk of connection failures and stale localStorage URLs causing routing issues
high.fix: Standardize API URL resolution logic, use shared WASI_API_URL from index.html, add fallback health checks

high.issue: DEX endpoints not fully verified in runtime checks
high.why: Orderbook, order placement, and cancellation endpoints untested could hide runtime issues
high.fix: Add runtime health checks for /api/v1/dex/orderbook/:symbol, /api/v1/dex/orders, and /api/v1/dex/orders/:orderId/cancel

medium.issue: Banking-only API detection logic may incorrectly filter valid URLs
medium.why: Could prevent connection to valid backend endpoints running on port 8010
medium.fix: Refine isLikelyBankingOnlyApi logic to be more permissive or add explicit API capability detection

medium.issue: No error boundaries for React component failures
medium.why: Component errors could crash entire app without graceful fallback
medium.fix: Add ErrorBoundary wrapper around RootApp component

low.issue: Hardcoded demo credentials in multiple files
low.why: Security risk and maintenance overhead for credential management
low.fix: Move credentials to environment variables or configuration file

low.why: Runtime errors could be caught at compile time with TypeScript
low.fix: Consider migrating to TypeScript gradually starting with API interfaces

missing_features.expected: Health checks for all DEX endpoints
missing_features.current: Only basic health and login endpoints checked

regressions.evidence: npm.cmd run build exitCode: -1, ok: false

next_actions: Fix npm build failure by investigating build logs and dependencies
Correct 'orderFor' typo to 'orderForm' in DEX order form select element
Standardize API URL resolution across DEX and WASI modules
Add runtime health checks for all DEX endpoints
Implement ErrorBoundary for better error handling
